title: 10 ineligible for the Oscars
wikitext:
[]: 1.0 ineligible for the Oscars             Quote Post by cyharding » Thu Oct 15, 2009 11:03 pm I don't know if anybody here actually cares, but I thought if anybody wanted to know, here it is.[url]www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2009-10-15/evangelion-1.0-ineligible-for-oscar-awards[/url] Just in case people did care, cyharding then jumped into the nearest ditch for safety.

[]:             Quote Post by MatrixRefugee » Thu Oct 15, 2009 11:16 pm ;: Growls, throws virtual bricks in the general direction of the mooks who run the Oscars::

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Thu Oct 15, 2009 11:21 pm Up! has my vote then.

[]:             Quote Post by cojax8 » Thu Oct 15, 2009 11:24 pm Huh, well, here’s to hoping that 2.0 will get nominated next time (as long as its not another 2 years before we get it of course)

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Thu Oct 15, 2009 11:53 pm 1. Oscars suck in general. 2. Blame it on Funimation. Apparently they had the whole film dubbed back in late 2008. Had they played the movie in one NYC/LA theater for a week then it would've been eligible. 3. Come on, let's be real. As a diehard Eva fan even I accept this truth but Evangelion 1.0 is nowhere near good enough to even be a Best Animation Film contender.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Oct 16, 2009 12:14 am Yea, like a stupid anime cartoon about giant robots and blue haired bitches would get an Oscar anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by etjusticepourtous » Fri Oct 16, 2009 12:20 am I wont even comment.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Oct 16, 2009 12:38 am MatrixRefugee wrote: ;: Growls, throws virtual bricks in the general direction of the mooks who run the Oscars::

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Fri Oct 16, 2009 12:39 am Because of scheduling issues that may or may not be FUNimation's fault, not because the Oscars said "Lol, no way are we going to even CONSIDER this."

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Fri Oct 16, 2009 12:44 am Gendo'sPapa wrote: 3. Come on, let's be real. As a diehard Eva fan even I accept this truth but Evangelion 1.0 is nowhere near good enough to even be a Best Animation Film contender.

[]:             Quote Post by etjusticepourtous » Fri Oct 16, 2009 1:38 am I think Hideaki Anno is an idiot, didn't he want recognition? Not in the famous sense, but o wow he made a master piece sense.

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Fri Oct 16, 2009 2:58 am etjusticepourtous wrote: I think Hideaki Anno is an idiot, didn't he want recognition? Not in the famous sense, but o wow he made a master piece sense.

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Fri Oct 16, 2009 3:43 am This year's best animated flick? Even if Eva 1.0 became a contender i would highly doubt that it would ever get a nod unless someone doped all the critics and judges when submitting their votes. For this particular year i would like to vouch for either Coraline or Ponyo. As much as I love what Pixar has done for us in the past this year's UP I feel wasnt really what it could have been. It had its moments...some really touching moments. But the dogs and the damn huge plot hole of how explorer Charles Muntz was able to nearly outlive our protagonist despite being 40 years his senior is just....ridiculous. It certainly wasnt a WALLE or Ratatouille. Thats for sure.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Oct 16, 2009 3:52 am Well, 1.0 did win other 'Best Animated Movie' awards (along with 'Best Director', if I remember correctly), so it wouldn't be surprising that, if eligible, it would win a category or two. Don't ask me for sauce; dig if you must.

[]:             Quote Post by Taekmkm » Fri Oct 16, 2009 5:11 am You're dumb if you think Evangelion even had a chance to be nominated even if it was eligible. Oscar animation is heavily dominated by American 3D animation, Pixar, Miyazaki, and Dreamworks.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri Oct 16, 2009 5:31 am Are you people actually surprised? I think Hideaki Anno is an idiot, didn't he want recognition? Not in the famous sense, but o wow he made a master piece sense.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Fri Oct 16, 2009 5:54 am I would love to see Rebuild 1.0 win an Oscar. Then both Matt Greenfield and Hideaki Anno could go up to collect the award, neither knowing who the other one is, suddenly a fight breaks out over who deserves the award more and Tiffany Grant hits Anno with a chair. My two cents.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri Oct 16, 2009 6:09 am That put an amusing image in my head.

[]:             Quote Post by Taekmkm » Fri Oct 16, 2009 8:11 am Only a few theaters here and there. 1.0 isn't even a standalone film also.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Oct 16, 2009 8:49 am Taekmkm wrote: You're dumb if you think Evangelion even had a chance to be nominated even if it was eligible. Oscar animation is heavily dominated by American 3D animation, Pixar, Miyazaki, and Dreamworks.
summary:
[]: 1.0 ineligible for the Oscars             Quote Post by cyharding » Thu Oct 15, 2009 11:03 pm I don't know if anybody here actually cares, but I thought if anybody wanted to know, here it is.[url]www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2009-10-15/evangelion-1.0-ineligible-for-oscar-awards[/url] Just in case people did care, cyharding then jumped into the nearest ditch for safety.
